plan.title: Unified Track Reference Architecture
plan.objective: Unify local and remote track handling so every interaction surface (play, like, queue, playlist, history, listen together) uses one materialized track contract and one response shape.
in_scope: Add backend and frontend primitives for remote materialization and unified track references.
TrackMappingService.ensureRemoteTrack, unified response normalizers, and frontend trackRef utilities with tests.
out_of_scope: This iteration does not add new providers beyond TIDAL and YouTube Music, does not change Subsonic remote filtering behavior, and does not alter vibe support for remote tracks.

tasks.summary: Phase 1 foundation: ensureRemoteTrack + unified track normalizers + frontend trackRef helpers
acceptance_criteria: New service and helper types are wired into backend/frontend build
Unit tests cover provider validation and normalization outputs
No existing local-track response regressions are introduced
tasks.outcome: Added TrackMappingService.ensureRemoteTrack with provider-coupled validation and idempotent materialization result contract.

tasks.summary: Phase 2 playlists: mixed-source add/remove/reorder with unified playlist item formatting
acceptance_criteria: Playlist POST supports exactly one identifier among local/tidal/youtube
Remote items materialize provider rows before playlist insertion
Frontend add/remove playlist flows send new reference payloads
tasks.outcome: Extended playlist add/delete/reorder backend contracts for mixed local/tidal/youtube entries and normalized response formatting. | Updated frontend playlist add flows to submit AddToPlaylistRef payloads with required remote metadata and enabled remote add-to-playlist surfaces.

tasks.summary: Phase 3 likes unification with LikedRemoteTrack and dedupe by TrackMapping
acceptance_criteria: Remote like/unlike writes to new FK-based table
GET /library/liked returns unified shape with source
Server-side dedupe collapses provider equivalents
tasks.outcome: Implemented FK-backed remote likes via LikedRemoteTrack and migrated like/unlike/read flows to unified source-aware payloads. | Added dedupe/preference handling for equivalent remote tracks through track mapping relationships in liked-track responses.

tasks.summary: Phase 4 plays/history: remote play logging and unified history response
acceptance_criteria: POST /plays accepts local or remote identifiers
GET /plays emits unified track contract
Frontend history displays provider badges for remote entries
tasks.outcome: Extended play logging to accept local/tidal/youtube references and materialize remote tracks before persistence. | Unified history responses now emit consistent source-aware track payloads for local and remote plays.

tasks.summary: Phase 5 queue/radio/vibe behavior for remote tracks
acceptance_criteria: Remote tracks can start radio via artist-name mode
Remote tracks are allowed for queue/add-to-playlist flows
Vibe controls remain internally guarded for remote tracks
tasks.outcome: Queue, play-now, and add-to-playlist flows now consume unified track references for local and remote tracks. | Remote radio/playback behavior is aligned through unified request shapes while preserving vibe-mode guardrails.

tasks.summary: Phase 6 listen-together per-user provider resolution and staleness handling
acceptance_criteria: Queue validation accepts mixed local/tidal/youtube inputs
Per-user availability emits unresolved reasons and auto-ready behavior
Background and reactive staleness marking are wired into workers/socket flows
tasks.outcome: Listen-together queue resolution now supports mixed-source items with per-user provider availability handling. | Track mapping staleness management is wired into worker startup for background refresh and resolution safety.

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals: Unify local and remote track handling so every interaction surface (play, like, queue, playlist, history, listen together) uses one materialized track contract and one response shape.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals: This iteration does not add new providers beyond TIDAL and YouTube Music, does not change Subsonic remote filtering behavior, and does not alter vibe support for remote tracks.

spec_outline.summary: Introduce materialized remote track entities plus a unified track response contract, then migrate consuming routes and frontend callers in phased order.

full_spec_outline.objective: Add backend and frontend primitives for remote materialization and unified track references.
full_spec_outline.deliverable: TrackMappingService.ensureRemoteTrack, unified response normalizers, and frontend trackRef utilities with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Remote track materialization is idempotent for TIDAL and YouTube IDs
Local and remote tracks normalize to one consistent contract
Frontend can derive API payloads for local and remote playlist/play actions

refined_spec.summary: Apply contract changes incrementally: first playlists and shared payloads, then likes/history, then listen-together resolution and staleness workflows.

full_refined_spec.decision: Ship the architecture in six phases with backward-compatible request shapes and additive schema changes.
full_refined_spec.rationale: This keeps rollout safe while replacing provider-specific ad-hoc payloads and enables endpoint-by-endpoint verification.
acceptance_criteria: Backward compatibility is preserved during transitions
Each phase includes targeted automated tests
Materialized provider rows become the source of truth for remote interactions

implementation_plan.summary: Implement Phase 1 and Phase 2 first, validate, then continue through likes, plays/history, radio/vibe gating, and listen-together resolution.

steps.deliverable: Phase 1 foundation: ensureRemoteTrack + unified track normalizers + frontend trackRef helpers
acceptance_criteria: New service and helper types are wired into backend/frontend build
Unit tests cover provider validation and normalization outputs
No existing local-track response regressions are introduced

delivered: Added TrackMappingService.ensureRemoteTrack with provider-coupled validation and idempotent materialization result contract.

steps.deliverable: Phase 2 playlists: mixed-source add/remove/reorder with unified playlist item formatting
acceptance_criteria: Playlist POST supports exactly one identifier among local/tidal/youtube
Remote items materialize provider rows before playlist insertion
Frontend add/remove playlist flows send new reference payloads

delivered: Extended playlist add/delete/reorder backend contracts for mixed local/tidal/youtube entries and normalized response formatting.
Updated frontend playlist add flows to submit AddToPlaylistRef payloads with required remote metadata and enabled remote add-to-playlist surfaces.

steps.deliverable: Phase 3 likes unification with LikedRemoteTrack and dedupe by TrackMapping
acceptance_criteria: Remote like/unlike writes to new FK-based table
GET /library/liked returns unified shape with source
Server-side dedupe collapses provider equivalents

delivered: Implemented FK-backed remote likes via LikedRemoteTrack and migrated like/unlike/read flows to unified source-aware payloads.
Added dedupe/preference handling for equivalent remote tracks through track mapping relationships in liked-track responses.
files_functions_hooks_touched: backend/src/routes/library.ts (liked routes and unified liked response formatting)

steps.deliverable: Phase 4 plays/history: remote play logging and unified history response
acceptance_criteria: POST /plays accepts local or remote identifiers
GET /plays emits unified track contract
Frontend history displays provider badges for remote entries

delivered: Extended play logging to accept local/tidal/youtube references and materialize remote tracks before persistence.
Unified history responses now emit consistent source-aware track payloads for local and remote plays.

steps.deliverable: Phase 5 queue/radio/vibe behavior for remote tracks
acceptance_criteria: Remote tracks can start radio via artist-name mode
Remote tracks are allowed for queue/add-to-playlist flows
Vibe controls remain internally guarded for remote tracks

delivered: Queue, play-now, and add-to-playlist flows now consume unified track references for local and remote tracks.
Remote radio/playback behavior is aligned through unified request shapes while preserving vibe-mode guardrails.

steps.deliverable: Phase 6 listen-together per-user provider resolution and staleness handling
acceptance_criteria: Queue validation accepts mixed local/tidal/youtube inputs
Per-user availability emits unresolved reasons and auto-ready behavior
Background and reactive staleness marking are wired into workers/socket flows

delivered: Listen-together queue resolution now supports mixed-source items with per-user provider availability handling.
Track mapping staleness management is wired into worker startup for background refresh and resolution safety.
